consensus_expectations: Street is mid-cycle-plus and one-sided bullish: FY2026 adj-EPS consensus $9.43 (raised to meet the $9.00-9.50 guide), FY2027 ~$10.89, FY26 EBITDA ~$3,997M; mean target price $142 (range $116-150, 9 analysts); FY26 EPS/EBITDA revision breadth 6-up/0-down. Notably the upgrade is margin/biofuel-led, with FY26 REVENUE estimates trimmed (4 down / 1 up).
market_implied_expectations: Reverse-DCF: at ~$123 the EV embeds only ~2.3% FCFF growth (~GDP) on a ~3.3% mid-cycle EBIT margin and ~0 years of above-GDP growth. So the price requires the post-RVO crush/biofuel recovery to HOLD at mid-cycle - not to peak, but not to retrace to the FY25 trough either.
engine_view: Base-case fair value ~$108 (Bull $140 / Base $108 / Bear $85); base case = mid-cycle holds (adj EBIT margin ~3.3-3.4%, FY26 EPS inside guide, FCFF normalizes ~$1.7-2.0bn as Viterra working capital unwinds), NO re-rating. Intrinsic mid-cycle FCFF DCF (45% weight) drives it because it both normalizes the cycle AND subtracts $13.7bn net debt; SOTP ~$90 confirms the low half.
what_everyone_knows: BG is a low-margin (~3-4%) ABCD commodity processor; Viterra closed mid-2025; the EPA RVO + global biofuel mandates are currently favorable; FY26 guidance was raised ~20%.
what_is_priced_in: Roughly mid-cycle economics holding + the favorable RVO + the raised guide (the Street has already moved up to meet it; price sits in a ~$123-126 band, mean target $142 above). ~GDP growth, no above-cycle re-rating, but no trough either.
what_market_may_be_missing: Nothing PROVEN. Two unproven candidates, opposite signs: (bear) through-cycle mean reversion + the way ~5.8x net leverage amplifies any EBIT decline at the EPS line, plus the rising Brazil indirect-tax tail; (bull) a multi-year biofuel super-cycle. Consensus ($142 target) is actually MORE bullish than the engine ($108 base FV) - so the engine's variant view is to be MORE cautious than the Street, on a through-cycle, leverage-aware basis.
evidence_engine_is_different: Weak/method-based, not informational. The engine sits below price because intrinsic DCF normalizes the crush cycle and subtracts $13.7bn net debt, and because ROIC 6.7% < 7.2% cost of equity makes the EV/EBITDA discount to ADM warranted (value trap), not mispricing. These are disciplined method choices an informed market could share - not differential information the market lacks. So there is no proven information edge; the difference vs the bullish Street is conservatism, supported by base rates.
base_rate_check: Supports the engine's caution: deep-cyclical commodity processors rarely sustain peak/elevated crush spreads 12m+ (BG's own segment EBIT swung $2,222M->$872M->$1,225M); a ~44%-dilutive leverage-tripling mega-deal creating per-share value on plan within 1-2 years is base-rate-low. The engine's below-consensus view respects the base rate; the Street's $142 target fights it.
notes: Direction: modestly OVERVALUED - engine base FV ~$108 is ~12.4% below the indicative ~$123 price, and consensus ($142 target) is more bullish still. But this is NOT a cleanly exploitable edge: variant perception is Weak (the engine's below-Street view is method-based conservatism, not proven differential information), the price is indicative/web-sourced not pool-verified, and the valuation methods disagree ~53% (forward-normalized ~$137 vs through-cycle DCF ~$105 vs SOTP ~$90). edge_score 32/100 = a real but low-conviction overvaluation read, no catalyst to force closure in-window. Fully consistent with the run's Watchlist / negative-expected-return call. The gap becomes exploitable (toward a short or a confident avoid) only if a pool price confirms >$120 AND a peer comps set or an FY26 cash miss hardens the through-cycle case; it inverts toward a long below ~$100. A gap without an edge is not a trade - it is a watch item.
